description: An unbounded `forever` loop run under a deliberately tiny instructionBudget (harness executeOptions, issue #195) halts with ol-limit/instruction-budget instead of running forever: the partial trace up to the cutoff is preserved (one completed print pass), never discarded. Closes the corpus gap noted at M1 close — ol-limit had zero execute-mode fixtures despite implementing all three execution-safety gates (spec/execution-model.md:551-557). The production-scale default budget (1,000,000) would make an exact-diff fixture impractically large, so this fixture opts into a small executeOptions.instructionBudget via the harness's new pass-through mechanism (scripts/harness/index.mjs); see packages/runtime/src/execution-budget.test.mjs for the full exceeded-limit matrix at unit-test granularity.
